description: §6.6.8 E-DERIVED-WRITE — reassignment to a `const <name>` derived cell after declaration. `const <doubled> = @count * 2` then a `@doubled = 5` write in an event handler is rejected: derived cells are reference-immutable. Incidental W-PROGRAM-* ignored by superset.
rationale: §6.6.8: the compiler SHALL reject any assignment to a `const <name>` identifier after its declaration, regardless of enclosing context (here an event handler). The fix-advice lists the upstream dependency `@count`.
